deck.author: OpenAI Codex
deck.subject: Browser studio for structured presentation authoring
deck.title: slideotter
deck.objective: Introduce new users to slideotter by showing how to choose a presentation, create or ground a deck, edit structured slides, attach materials, review generated options, validate output, archive the intended PDF, and understand the current product state.
deck.constraints: Keep slide copy concise, vary layouts across the tour, and explain the active project without filler or implementation-history detours.
deck.themeBrief: Clean local-workbench presentation with friendly onboarding language, varied information layouts, and restrained technical detail.
deck.outline:
1. slideotter
2. What you will learn
3. The problem and the loop
4. Why decks get messy
5. A simple authoring loop
6. Author in the workbench
7. Choose a presentation first
8. Give the deck a brief and sources
9. Slides are readable JSON
10. Edit with the slide in view
11. Attach images as materials
12. One renderer keeps previews honest
13. Review before applying
14. Generate options, not surprises
15. Compare before applying
16. Plan the deck from Slide Studio
17. Use chat for bounded tasks
18. Guardrails and handoff
19. Know what can change
20. Run checks before sharing
21. Archive the version you mean to share
22. How to read the project
23. Current architecture bets
24. Build new decks in stages
25. Creation is staged
26. Brief captures just enough
27. Approve structure before slides
28. Regenerate only weak beats
29. Draft one slide at a time
30. Theme after content
31. Sources join the outline
32. Make the staged flow real
33. Draft state survives restarts
34. Validate the staged path
35. Keep the workbench sturdy
36. The basic habit

slide-01.title: slideotter
slide-01.intent: A local workbench for structured presentations that stay grounded, reviewable, and easy to publish.
slide-01.mustInclude: A local workbench for structured presentations that stay grounded, reviewable, and easy to publish.
slide-01.notes: Role: opening. Establish the product promise and the local-workbench framing.

slide-02.title: What you will learn
slide-02.intent: Choose a deck, ground it, review changes, and ship a checked archive.
slide-02.mustInclude: Choose a deck, ground it, review changes, and ship a checked archive.
slide-02.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-03.title: The problem and the loop
slide-03.intent: Explain The problem and the loop.
slide-03.notes: Role: concept. Use as a section boundary; keep the visible copy minimal.

slide-04.title: Why decks get messy
slide-04.intent: Deck work gets hard when source, preview, and final PDF stop agreeing.
slide-04.mustInclude: Deck work gets hard when source, preview, and final PDF stop agreeing.
slide-04.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-05.title: A simple authoring loop
slide-05.intent: Most work follows four steps and leaves readable local state.
slide-05.mustInclude: Most work follows four steps and leaves readable local state.
slide-05.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-06.title: Author in the workbench
slide-06.notes: Role: concept. Use as a section boundary; keep the visible copy minimal.

slide-07.title: Choose a presentation first
slide-07.intent: slideotter manages multiple local decks with visual first-slide cards.
slide-07.mustInclude: slideotter manages multiple local decks with visual first-slide cards.
slide-07.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-08.title: Give the deck a brief and sources
slide-08.intent: Before generation, state the goal and trusted material.
slide-08.mustInclude: Before generation, state the goal and trusted material.
slide-08.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-09.title: Slides are readable JSON
slide-09.intent: Each structured slide is a compact JSON file. The renderer turns those fields into the slide you inspect in the studio.
slide-09.mustInclude: Each structured slide is a compact JSON file. The renderer turns those fields into the slide you inspect in the studio.
slide-09.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-10.title: Edit with the slide in view
slide-10.intent: The main preview is the working surface. Text and JSON edits update it immediately.
slide-10.mustInclude: The main preview is the working surface. Text and JSON edits update it immediately.
slide-10.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-11.title: Attach images as materials
slide-11.intent: Upload, search, or provide starter images. Materials stay with the deck and carry source information.
slide-11.mustInclude: Upload, search, or provide starter images. Materials stay with the deck and carry source information.
slide-11.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-12.title: One renderer keeps previews honest
slide-12.intent: The studio avoids separate preview and export logic. The same DOM slide powers authoring, comparison, validation, and PDF output.
slide-12.mustInclude: The studio avoids separate preview and export logic. The same DOM slide powers authoring, comparison, validation, and PDF output.
slide-12.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-13.title: Review before applying
slide-13.notes: Role: concept. Use as a section boundary; keep the visible copy minimal.

slide-14.title: Generate options, not surprises
slide-14.intent: Generation creates review candidates, reports status, and keeps the current slide unchanged until Apply.
slide-14.mustInclude: Generation creates review candidates, reports status, and keeps the current slide unchanged until Apply.
slide-14.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-15.title: Compare before applying
slide-15.intent: When candidates exist, the compare workspace becomes the decision surface: current slide, candidate slide, changed fields, and apply buttons.
slide-15.mustInclude: When candidates exist, the compare workspace becomes the decision surface: current slide, candidate slide, changed fields, and apply buttons.
slide-15.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-16.title: Plan the deck above slide level
slide-16.intent: The Outline drawer keeps sources, reusable plans, length scaling, and deck-context proposals beside the active slide.
slide-16.mustInclude: The Outline drawer keeps sources, reusable plans, length scaling, and deck-context proposals beside the active slide.
slide-16.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-17.title: Use chat for bounded tasks
slide-17.intent: In Slide Studio, chat launches bounded workflows and keeps history in a Log tab.
slide-17.mustInclude: In Slide Studio, chat launches bounded workflows and keeps history in a Log tab.
slide-17.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-18.title: Guardrails and handoff
slide-18.notes: Role: concept. Use as a section boundary; keep the visible copy minimal.

slide-19.title: Know what can change
slide-19.intent: The server keeps writes narrow. Browser actions request specific mutations instead of giving the UI broad filesystem access.
slide-19.mustInclude: The server keeps writes narrow. Browser actions request specific mutations instead of giving the UI broad filesystem access.
slide-19.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-20.title: Run checks before sharing
slide-20.intent: Validation stays subtle in the UI, but it is the final safety net before a PDF becomes the shared version.
slide-20.mustInclude: Validation stays subtle in the UI, but it is the final safety net before a PDF becomes the shared version.
slide-20.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-21.title: Archive the version you mean to share
slide-21.intent: Building and archiving are separate. Build creates current output; archive update copies the presentation PDF only when you intend to publish it.
slide-21.mustInclude: Building and archiving are separate. Build creates current output; archive update copies the presentation PDF only when you intend to publish it.
slide-21.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-22.title: How to read the project
slide-22.intent: If you are new to the codebase, start with the folder that matches the change.
slide-22.mustInclude: If you are new to the codebase, start with the folder that matches the change.
slide-22.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-23.title: Current architecture bets
slide-23.intent: The useful path is still structured reach, but several bets are now real product surfaces.
slide-23.mustInclude: The useful path is still structured reach, but several bets are now real product surfaces.
slide-23.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-24.title: Build new decks in stages
slide-24.intent: Explain Build new decks in stages.
slide-24.notes: Role: concept. Use as a section boundary; keep the visible copy minimal.

slide-25.title: Creation is staged
slide-25.intent: New decks start with a brief, move through an editable outline, then draft validated slides progressively.
slide-25.mustInclude: New decks start with a brief, move through an editable outline, then draft validated slides progressively.
slide-25.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-26.title: Brief captures just enough
slide-26.intent: The first stage asks for the deck intent, not every styling and sourcing choice at once.
slide-26.mustInclude: The first stage asks for the deck intent, not every styling and sourcing choice at once.
slide-26.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-27.title: Approve structure before slides
slide-27.intent: The outline is the first generated artifact. Users can correct the story before full slide specs exist.
slide-27.mustInclude: The outline is the first generated artifact. Users can correct the story before full slide specs exist.
slide-27.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-28.title: Regenerate only weak beats
slide-28.intent: A good outline rarely needs a full reset. The flow should let users keep strong beats and repair the rest.
slide-28.mustInclude: A good outline rarely needs a full reset. The flow should let users keep strong beats and repair the rest.
slide-28.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-29.title: Draft one slide at a time
slide-29.intent: After approval, slideotter materializes slide specs sequentially and keeps partial progress recoverable.
slide-29.mustInclude: After approval, slideotter materializes slide specs sequentially and keeps partial progress recoverable.
slide-29.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-30.title: Theme after content
slide-30.intent: Theme choices are easier to judge against real slides than against an empty creation form.
slide-30.mustInclude: Theme choices are easier to judge against real slides than against an empty creation form.
slide-30.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-31.title: Sources join the outline
slide-31.intent: Sources and materials should enrich the structure stage, especially when evidence belongs to a specific beat.
slide-31.mustInclude: Sources and materials should enrich the structure stage, especially when evidence belongs to a specific beat.
slide-31.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-32.title: Make the staged flow real
slide-32.intent: Explain Make the staged flow real.
slide-32.notes: Role: concept. Use as a section boundary; keep the visible copy minimal.

slide-33.title: Draft state survives restarts
slide-33.intent: Creation should feel safe to pause. Draft choices live in ignored runtime state until a deck is actually created.
slide-33.mustInclude: Creation should feel safe to pause. Draft choices live in ignored runtime state until a deck is actually created.
slide-33.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-34.title: Validate the staged path
slide-34.intent: Browser coverage now exercises creation, materials, length scaling, duplicate, and delete through the real UI.
slide-34.mustInclude: Browser coverage now exercises creation, materials, length scaling, duplicate, and delete through the real UI.
slide-34.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-35.title: Keep the workbench sturdy
slide-35.intent: The main surfaces exist now. Next work should come from real decks and concrete maintenance friction.
slide-35.mustInclude: The main surfaces exist now. Next work should come from real decks and concrete maintenance friction.
slide-35.notes: Role: concept. Keep the slide focused on this beat and avoid drifting into implementation detail.

slide-36.title: The basic habit
slide-36.intent: Keep the story, sources, materials, preview, review, validation, and archive in one local loop.
slide-36.mustInclude: Keep the story, sources, materials, preview, review, validation, and archive in one local loop.
slide-36.notes: Role: handoff. Close with the basic repeatable habit for maintaining and sharing a deck.
